question: Using the Java client command above, which process definition version is selected when starting the new instance?

options.text: The most recently deployed version of the process definition whose id is `someProcess`.
Version 1 of the process definition with id `someProcess`.
One instance is started per deployed version of `someProcess`.
The latest version across every deployed process, regardless of the `bpmnProcessId`.

a.text: The Java client example pairs `.bpmnProcessId("someProcess")` with `.latestVersion()`, which targets the most recently deployed version of that specific process definition.

b.text: `.latestVersion()` is the opposite of pinning to a specific version like 1; it always picks the latest deployment.

c.text: `newCreateInstanceCommand` starts a single instance — there is no fan-out across versions.

d.text: `.bpmnProcessId(...)` scopes the lookup to one process definition id; the `.latestVersion()` modifier is local to that id.

explanation: `newCreateInstanceCommand().bpmnProcessId(...).latestVersion()` is the documented pattern for starting an instance of the latest deployed version of a named process. `variables(...)` then attaches the initial process variables, and `.send()` dispatches the command to the broker.

docs.title: Connecting the workflow engine with your world
